title: Euphorbia cornastra
taxonomy: Euphorbia cornastra is classified within the plant family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family consists of over 300 genera and approximately 7,000 species. Euphorbia cornastra, like other members of the Euphorbiaceae family, is a flowering plant characterized by its succulent growth habit and often toxic sap. The genus Euphorbia contains around 2,000 species, including trees, shrubs, and succulents. The classification of Euphorbia cornastra reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Euphorbiaceae family.

intro: Euphorbia cornastra, also known as Corn-Headed Spurge, is a succulent plant species native to the Eastern Cape Province in South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Euphorbia cornastra include flavonoids, terpenoids, and cardenolides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
